[PUBLISHED DATE]: Feb. 15, 2004
[TITLE]: Trump campaign reportedly shares 2004 photo of Air Force One at Daytona, appearing to say it was from 2020 | FOX 29 Philadelphia
[METADATA/INTRO]: Trump campaign reportedly shares 2004 photo of Air Force One at Daytona, appearing to say it was from 2020 DAYTONA BEACH, Fla. - President Donald Trump’s campaign manager Brad Parscale reportedly tweeted, then deleted a 2004 picture of Air Force...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Given the honor of grand marshal for the race, Trump gave thousands of fans a flyover of Air Force One and then rode onto the track in the presidential motorcade as the audience roared with delight. The motorcade took roughly a quarter lap before pulling aside to an infield staging area. 
 The Associated Press contributed to this report. This story was reported from Los Angeles.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2020-02-17
[TITLE]: A Uniquely American Moment: Photographer Captures Air Force One at Daytona 500 In These Stunning Images - The Aviationist
[METADATA/INTRO]: Master Aviation Photographer Matt Haskell Got Incredible Photos of Air Force One at the Daytona 500 NASCAR Race. It was a uniquely American moment: the USAF Boeing VC-25A of the 89th Airlift Wing, Presidential Airlift Group (PAG) from Andrews AFB...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Master Aviation Photographer Matt Haskell Got Incredible Photos of Air Force One at the Daytona 500 NASCAR Race. 
 It was a uniquely American moment: the USAF Boeing VC-25A of the 89th Airlift Wing, Presidential Airlift Group (PAG) from Andrews AFB in Maryland with U.S. President Donald Trump on board and flying as “Air Force One”, the callsign when the President is on board the aircraft, performed a flyover (that was filmed from above) and landed at Daytona Beach International Airport next to Daytona International Speedway in Florida, on Feb. 16, 2020. 
 The flight delivered the U.S. President and the First Lady to the Speedway racetrack so President Trump could preside over the Daytona 500 NASCAR stock car race as the race’s official Grand Marshall and starter.
